sections.title: 1. Purpose
sections.section_prompt: State that this SAD partitions {device_name_model} into software items, identifies SOUP, and shows how each software requirement from {ref:SW-002} is realised. Required for IEC 62304 Class {software_safety_class}.

sections.title: 2. Architectural Principles (IEC 62304 §5.3)
clauses: 2.1 Transform software requirements into an architecture that describes software items
2.2 Develop architecture for the interfaces of software items (external and internal)
2.3 Specify functional and performance requirements of SOUP items
2.4 Specify system hardware and software required by SOUP items
2.5 Identify segregation between software items necessary for risk control
sections.section_prompt: For each principle, describe how it is realised for {device_name_model}. Highlight any segregation between safety-critical and non-safety-critical items.

sections.title: 3. Software Items Inventory
sections.ai_rows_prompt: Generate 8–12 software items for {device_name_model}. Mix internal items (UI, business logic, data layer, hardware abstraction, alarm engine, audit log) and SOUP (OS, web framework, database, crypto library). Type one of Internal|SOUP. Item class ≤ system class {software_safety_class}; justify any reduction in detailed_design notes via item name. Return JSON array.

sections.title: 4. SOUP Register
sections.ai_rows_prompt: List 5–8 plausible SOUP components used by {device_name_model} (OS, language runtime, web framework, database, crypto/TLS library, HTTP server, image codec). Anomaly monitoring describes how CVEs and bug lists are tracked (e.g. NVD subscription, supplier advisories, Dependabot). Return JSON array.

sections.title: 5. Architecture Diagrams
sections.section_prompt: Describe the layered architecture, deployment topology, and data-flow of {device_name_model} in prose suitable to accompany diagrams stored alongside this document. Reference where diagrams are maintained ({ref:HI-002} for HIMS data flow if applicable).
